title: melaleuca quadrifaria
intro: Melaleuca quadrifaria, also known as Broad-leaved Paperbark, is a species of plant within the myrtle family, Myrtaceae. The plant is traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The essential oil extracted from Melaleuca quadrifaria has been found to have health benefits, including wound healing, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, making it effective in treating skin conditions and infections. The herbal preparations of Melaleuca quadrifaria include tea, tinctures, and essential oil, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
